title: Flask dev server launched with debug=True bound to 0.0.0.0
evidence: app.py:232 app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=5001); app.log confirms 'Debugger is active! Debugger PIN: 581-443-168'
suggestion: Disable debug mode for any non-local run and serve via a production WSGI server; the Werkzeug debugger allows arbitrary code execution.

title: README port and base URL (5000) do not match the code (5001)
evidence: README.md states 'available at http://localhost:5000' and all curl examples use :5000, but app.py:232 binds port=5001
suggestion: Align README examples with the actual port (5001) or make the port configurable.

title: README lists aiosqlite dependency that the code does not use; no requirements.txt
evidence: README.md 'pip install flask aiosqlite' and Requirements list 'aiosqlite', but app.py imports the stdlib 'sqlite3' (app.py:1) and no aiosqlite/async code exists
suggestion: Drop aiosqlite from the install instructions and add a requirements.txt pinning just Flask.

title: Missing/invalid JSON body on POST/PUT yields 500 instead of a 400 validation error
evidence: app.py:58 data=request.get_json() then app.py:61 validate_book_data(data); a bodyless or non-JSON POST makes get_json() return None/raise, hitting the generic except -> 500 rather than the intended 400
suggestion: Guard get_json(silent=True) and return 400 when the body is absent or unparseable before validation.
